name: Hot Sour Hunan Veggies
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons fermented black beans, coarsely chopped (Chinese)
3/4 pound cauliflower florets, cut into walnut-sized pieces
1/2 pound peeled carrots, cut diagonally into 1/8 inch coins
1 pound slender green and golden zucchini, cut into 1/4 inch rounds
1 tablespoon cornstarch dissolved in 1 1/2 tablespoons water
recipeInstructions:
1. In a small bowl, combine ginger, garlic, black beans, and red pepper flakes. Cover and set aside.
2. In a second bowl, blend vegetable stock, soy sauce, vinegar, and sugar together.
3. Blanch cauliflower in boiling water for 1 minute, then plunge into ice water. Repeat with remaining vegetables, allowing the pot of water to return to boil before adding more vegetables. Blanch carrots for 15 seconds and zucchini for 5 seconds. Drain well and place in separate bowls.
4. Heat a wok or large heavy skillet over high heat until hot enough that a drop of water sizzles on contact. Add about 2 tablespoons water and swirl to glaze the pan. Reduce heat to moderately high, then add ginger mixture and stir gently until fragrant (20-30 seconds).
5. Add cauliflower and toss for 2 minutes. Add carrots and toss for 1.5 minutes. Add zucchini and toss for 30 seconds.
6. Stir in the vegetable stock mixture and add to the pan, stirring to combine. Bring to a simmer, then cover and cook until vegetables are crisp-tender (about 30-60 seconds).
7. Stir in the cornstarch mixture and add to the pan, stirring until sauce turns glossy and slightly thick (10-20 seconds). Serve immediately on a heated platter, garnished with scallion rings and accompanied by noodles or rice. Serves 4-6.
